tldr: For the generic-noise additive-noise SCM, this proposal treats NoGAM's residual-score discrepancy as an estimable causal separation rather than as a population-only leaf certificate. It targets a pruning-uniform nonasymptotic topological-order theorem, with error controlled jointly by the residual-score margin gamma, score/residual nuisance accuracy, and sampling noise. The unresolved contribution is a matching minimax lower bound: whether gamma is the intrinsic generic-noise order-recovery scale, or whether estimating the residual-score regression imposes an unavoidable additional modulus.

symbols.name: G
symbols.def: causal graph with parent sets pa_G(j) and leaf set leaf(G)

symbols.name: p_{S}
symbols.def: strictly positive density of the marginal law P_S of X_S

symbols.name: mathfrak S(G)
symbols.def: all nonempty vertex sets obtainable from V by finitely many deletions of leaves of the induced DAG

symbols.name: S
symbols.def: a current vertex set in the leaf-pruning recursion

symbols.name: j
symbols.def: candidate coordinate at pruning stage S

symbols.name: R_{j,S}
symbols.def: X_j-E_P[X_j|X_{S minus {j}}] for j in S

symbols.name: gamma
symbols.def: uniform lower margin for Q_{j,S}(P) at non-leaves

symbols.name: beta
symbols.def: Hölder smoothness index used for nuisance-function estimability

symbols.name: Z_1,...,Z_n
symbols.def: independent draws with Z_i distributed as P

symbols.name: widehat s_{j,S}^{(-k)}, widehat r_{j,S}^{(-k)}, widehat m_{j,S}^{(-k)}
symbols.def: fold-k training estimates of s_{j,S}, the conditional mean of X_j given X_{S minus {j}}, and m_{j,S}, respectively

symbols.name: widehat Q_{j,S}
symbols.def: cross-fitted empirical residual-score loss for j in S

symbols.name: widehat pi_n
symbols.def: iterative argmin_j widehat Q_{j,S} order estimator

symbols.name: pi_G
symbols.def: all permutations respecting every edge of G

assumptions.condition: P is generated by X_j=f_j(X_pa_G(j))+U_j for j in V, where (U_j)_{j in V} are mutually independent and E_P[U_j]=0.
Each U_j has a strictly positive C^2 density on R.
For every edge k->j in G, f_j is nonlinear in x_k on every nonempty open subset of the support of X_pa_G(j).
For every S in mathfrak S(G) and every j in S minus leaf(G[S]), Q_{j,S}(P)>=gamma.

novel.justification: The condition indexes a genuine generic-noise separation: it is positive whenever a non-leaf score retains variation not predictable from its leave-one-coordinate residual. It permits heterogeneous non-Gaussian innovations and nonlinear mechanisms, as in the residual-score population certificate, while allowing the margin to shrink with difficult mechanisms.

assumptions.condition: For every S in mathfrak S(G) and j in S, the centered variables s_{j,S}(X_S)-E_P[s_{j,S}(X_S)] and m_{j,S}(R_{j,S})-E_P[m_{j,S}(R_{j,S})] have psi_2 norm at most B.
For every S in mathfrak S(G) and j in S, s_{j,S}, x_{S minus {j}} maps to E_P[X_j|X_{S minus {j}}], and m_{j,S} belong to their respective beta-Hölder balls of radius L on their supports.

For every S in mathfrak S(G) and j in S, the score s_{j,S}, residual regression x_{S minus {j}} maps to E_P[X_j|X_{S minus {j}}], and residual score regression m_{j,S} are L4(P)-estimable by sample-split measurable functions at rate a_n uniformly over the class.

definitions.name: P_d(beta,L,B,gamma)
definitions.construction: {P : P satisfies ass:independent-anm, ass:positive-smooth-noise, ass:restricted-nonlinearity, ass:residual-score-margin, ass:subgaussian-score-envelope, ass:holder-nuisance-smoothness, and ass:nuisance-estimability for the displayed d,beta,L,B,gamma}

definitions.name: residual-score leaf functional
definitions.construction: For S in mathfrak S(G) and j in S, set R_{j,S}=X_j-E_P[X_j|X_{S minus {j}}], m_{j,S}(r)=E_P[s_{j,S}(X_S)|R_{j,S}=r], and Q_{j,S}(P)=E_P[(s_{j,S}(X_S)-m_{j,S}(R_{j,S}))^2].

definitions.name: cross-fitted residual-score estimator
definitions.construction: For each S in mathfrak S(G), j in S, and fold k in {1,...,K}, fit sample-split L4 rate realizers widehat s_{j,S}^{(-k)}, widehat r_{j,S}^{(-k)}, and widehat m_{j,S}^{(-k)} on observations outside fold k; for i in fold k set widehat Q_{j,S}=n^{-1} sum_{k=1}^K sum_{i in fold k}[widehat s_{j,S}^{(-k)}(Z_{i,S})-widehat m_{j,S}^{(-k)}(Z_{ij}-widehat r_{j,S}^{(-k)}(Z_{i,S minus {j}}))]^2.

definitions.name: iterated residual-score order
definitions.construction: Initialize S_0=V; for t=0,...,d-1 choose widehat j_t in argmin_{j in S_t} widehat Q_{j,S_t}, set S_{t+1}=S_t minus {widehat j_t}, and define widehat pi_n=(widehat j_{d-1},...,widehat j_0).

definitions.name: generic-noise lower-bound handle
definitions.construction: Construct a bivariate additive-noise family with bounded smooth innovation scores and a localized nonlinear mechanism perturbation, then relate its residual-score discrepancy Q_{j,S}(P) to the Hellinger distance between opposite-direction observational laws.

estimand_functional: pi_G = iterative argmin_{j in S} Q_{j,S}(P), where Q_{j,S}(P)=E_P[(s_{j,S}(X_S)-E_P[s_{j,S}(X_S)|R_{j,S}])^2]

sampling_model.design: iid observational sampling with K-fold cross-fitting
sampling_model.units: Z_1,...,Z_n iid from P in P_d(beta,L,B,gamma)
sampling_model.treatment: none; causal order is identified from the additive-noise observational law
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: sample-split L4 estimators of marginal score, leave-one-coordinate residual regression, and residual-score regression at uniform rate a_n

statements.statement: For every P in P_d(beta,L,B,gamma), every S in mathfrak S(G), and every j in S, Q_{j,S}(P)=0 if and only if j is a leaf of G[S]. Consequently, the population iterative argmin of Q_{j,S}(P) returns an element of pi_G.
statements.justification: This states the population bridge from arbitrary innovation densities to a causal leaf functional that can be carried through pruning.
statements.gap: Montagna et al. give the residual-score idea for NoGAM; the proposed formulation is pruning-uniform and is the exact population target required for a finite-sample margin analysis.
statements.consumer: It licenses a topological-order functional for applications with non-Gaussian measurement shocks, such as asymmetric demand or reporting errors.

statements.statement: There are constants c,C>0 depending only on K such that, for every P in P_d(beta,L,B,gamma), if a_n<=gamma/C, then R_n(P)<=C d a_n/gamma + 2d exp{-c n gamma^2/B^4}. In particular, if a_n/gamma->0 and n gamma^2/B^4-log d->infinity, then sup_{P in P_d(beta,L,B,gamma)}R_n(P)->0.
statements.justification: The target separates the irreducible empirical fluctuation of a residual-score loss from the three nuisance tasks that NoGAM leaves implicit.
statements.gap: NoGAM establishes generic-noise ordering at the population/algorithmic level, while Kpotufe et al. give broad ANM consistency conditions rather than a residual-score margin bound with an explicit high-dimensional pruning error.
statements.consumer: The inequality yields a sample-size rule for deploying generic-noise ordering before downstream DAG pruning or interventional distribution estimation.

statements.statement: If every U_j is Gaussian, then for a leaf j of G[S], s_{j,S}(X_S) is an affine function of R_{j,S}; hence Q_{j,S}(P)=0. The residual-score functional therefore retains the leaf direction selected by SCORE's zero diagonal-score-Jacobian variance criterion, although its non-leaf margin is a different object.
statements.justification: This reduction checks that the generic-noise functional does not contradict the familiar Gaussian leaf certificate.
statements.gap: Rolland et al. use a diagonal score-Jacobian variance under Gaussian noise; this proposition only establishes compatibility of the leaf implication, not an equivalence of margins.
statements.consumer: It permits practitioners to use one residual-score target across datasets where Gaussianity is plausible in some regimes and rejected in others.

statements.statement: Over P_d(beta,L,B,gamma), what is the minimax order-recovery risk as gamma downarrow 0, and what smoothness-dependent nuisance modulus is necessary in addition to n gamma^2 asymptotically comparable to log d for law-independent order estimators?
statements.justification: The upper bound alone does not determine whether the residual-score margin is intrinsic or whether it is an artifact of estimating a nested conditional expectation.
statements.gap: Neither NoGAM nor generic ANM consistency theory supplies a minimax local modulus for residual-score ordering under arbitrary noise.
statements.consumer: A matching frontier determines whether investment should target score learning, residual learning, or an entirely different generic-noise order statistic.

bibliography.citation: Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., Zhang, K., and Locatello, F. (2023). Causal Discovery with Score Matching on Additive Models with Arbitrary Noise. CLeaR, PMLR 213, 726-751.
Peters, J., Mooij, J. M., Janzing, D., and Schölkopf, B. (2014). Causal Discovery with Continuous Additive Noise Models. Journal of Machine Learning Research 15, 2009-2053.
Kpotufe, S., Sgouritsa, E., Janzing, D., and Schölkopf, B. (2014). Consistency of Causal Inference under the Additive Noise Model. ICML, PMLR 32, 478-486.
Rolland, P., Cevher, V., Kleindessner, M., Russell, C., Schölkopf, B., Janzing, D., and Locatello, F. (2022). SCORE: Approximating Causal Structure from Score Matching. ICML, PMLR 162.
Zhu, Z., Locatello, F., and Cevher, V. (2023). Sample Complexity of Score Matching for Causal Discovery. NeurIPS 36.
Vershynin, R. (2018). High-Dimensional Probability. Cambridge University Press.
Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., and Locatello, F. (2023). Shortcuts for Causal Discovery of Nonlinear Models by Score Matching. arXiv:2310.14246.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: NoGAM identifies a generic-noise topological ordering by predicting score entries from estimated residuals.
Continuous additive-noise models can identify the DAG from the observational distribution under mild conditions.
A family of ANM causal-direction procedures is consistent under general nonparametric conditions.
SCORE identifies Gaussian-ANM leaves using zero variance of a diagonal score-Jacobian entry.
Finite-sample SCORE analysis controls Gaussian-noise order recovery through a score-Jacobian curvature margin.
Score-sortability characterizes a separate score-variance pattern in nonlinear bivariate causal models.

project_justification.gap: Generic-noise NoGAM supplies a population residual-score leaf device, but it does not isolate the causal separation that governs finite-sample error after score, residual, and conditional-score prediction are all estimated.
project_justification.niche: A residual-score margin creates a generic-noise counterpart to Gaussian SCORE's curvature margin without importing the diagonal-score-Jacobian identity that fails under non-Gaussian shocks.
project_justification.fill: The project targets a pruning-uniform finite-sample recovery bound and asks whether its two scales, a_n/gamma and n gamma^2, are minimax necessary through an explicit opposite-direction ANM testing construction.

novelty_justification: The nearest result is Montagna et al.'s NoGAM population/algorithmic generic-noise ordering rule. The proposed object is different: a pruning-uniform residual-score margin Q_{j,S}, a nonasymptotic error theorem that exposes its interaction with three nuisance estimators, and a local minimax question for that margin. Peters et al. and Kpotufe et al. establish qualitative identification or consistency, not this residual-score separation frontier.
related_work: Montagna et al. (2023, NoGAM) replace Gaussian SCORE's leaf statistic by prediction of a score entry from a residual under arbitrary additive noise; they do not give a residual-score-margin finite-sample or minimax frontier. Peters et al. (2014) establish qualitative continuous-ANM DAG identifiability, but do not quantify the distance of a non-leaf from residual-score measurability. Kpotufe et al. (2014) give nonparametric consistency conditions for ANM direction procedures, not a pruning-uniform bound for a nested residual-score loss. Rolland et al. (2022) identify Gaussian-ANM leaves from diagonal score-Jacobian variation; the present functional reduces compatibly at a Gaussian leaf but does not extend that Jacobian criterion. Zhu, Locatello, and Cevher (2023) analyze finite-sample Gaussian SCORE through a curvature margin, whereas the present target concerns arbitrary-noise residual-score separation. Montagna et al. (2023, ScoreSort) study a distinct score-sortability pattern, whose relationship to the minimax residual-score margin is left open.
honest_scope: The finite-sample theorem is conditional on a pruning-uniform residual-score margin and uniform nuisance estimability; it does not claim that these primitives hold for every additive-noise model. The proposed Gaussian reduction is a leaf-level compatibility result, not equality between NoGAM and SCORE margins. The minimax lower bound, the least-favorable generic-noise family, and whether a_n^2 is an unavoidable adaptation price remain open. No claim is made about the exhausted Gaussian mirror-path Chernoff kernel or about coefficient-one efficiency of SCORE.
technical_internal_limitation: The residual-score discrepancy is nested: it changes when the marginal score, the leave-one-coordinate residual, or the score-on-residual regression is perturbed. A proof of the displayed bound must therefore establish a cross-fitted loss-stability inequality rather than treat Q_{j,S} as an observed mean. This is a diagnostic for the proposed estimator, not a claim about NoGAM's published implementation.
